zh: 现在可能会在周围加上漂亮的词藻，漂亮的创意和所有东西，但它是在讲述真相。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so when I watched Steve prepare for, and like I said, when we did the two and a half years with the iPhone, he was honing the story of the iPhone every day.
zh: 所以当我看着 Steve 为……做准备时，就像我说的，当我们做 iPhone 的那两年半时，他每天都在打磨 iPhone 的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: He didn't give it to marketing.
zh: 他没有把它交给营销。

speaker: Unknown
en: He knew what that thing was and what those key features were like we talked about and micromanage those features because he knew those were the things the world's going to take notice to.
zh: 他知道那个东西是什么，那些关键功能是什么，就像我们谈到的那样，并微观管理那些功能，因为他知道那些是世界上会注意到的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he would refine the story and then tell other people who were unwashed by it, like friends who were really smart and say, "I'm going to give you the pitch and give you the pitch."
zh: 他会完善这个故事，然后告诉其他那些没有被洗脑的人，比如那些非常聪明的朋友，并说，“我要给你讲讲推销辞令，给你讲讲推销辞令。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And he would refine, refine, refine.
zh: 然后他会完善，完善，完善。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so when you saw him come on stage, it was just because he had done it 100,000 times or at least 10,000 times, right?
zh: 所以当你看到他走上舞台时，那只是因为他已经做了 10 万次或者至少 1 万次，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And he knew it like boom, boom, boom, boom, boom, it just came off.
zh: 他对此了如指掌，就像这样，这样，这样，这样，它就自然流露出来了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I watched and I learned from that and that's what we did for like the Nest Learning Thermostat.
zh: 所以我观察并从中学习，这就是我们为 Nest Learning Thermostat 所做的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's what we did for each of those things.
zh: 这就是我们为每件事所做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was just that same storytelling over and over, why does it matter?
zh: 就是一次又一次地讲同样的故事，这为什么重要？

speaker: Unknown
en: Why does it matter?
zh: 为什么这很重要？

speaker: Unknown
en: And because too many times when we're technology led, we talk about the what.
zh: 因为很多时候，当我们以技术为主导时，我们谈论的是“是什么”。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't talk about the why.
zh: 我们不谈论“为什么”。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the why is where the storytelling is because you want to take a journey of why it matters to you.
zh: 而“为什么”就是故事所在，因为你要去探索一段关于它为什么对你重要的旅程。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're talking about the what, you're just talking to another geek.
zh: 如果你谈论的是“是什么”，你只是在和另一个极客对话。

speaker: Unknown
en: Love geeks, no problem.
zh: 爱极客们，没问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm one of them.
zh: 我就是其中之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we can relate on that level, but general, most people who are not in the technology world, they want a story and they want something that touches them in some way.
zh: 我们可以在那个层面上产生共鸣，但总的来说，大多数不在科技界的人，他们想要一个故事，他们想要某种能以某种方式触动他们的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: One lesson I'm hearing from what you just described is telling the story over and over and over and refining it over time is where the best stuff comes from, not just here, I just came up with this and it's going to be amazing.
zh: 我从你刚才的描述中听到的一个教训是，一遍又一遍地讲故事并随着时间的推移完善它，就是最好的东西来源，而不仅仅是“嘿，我刚想出了这个，它会很神奇”。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, absolutely.
zh: 哦，绝对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And telling it to other people to see if it resonates.
zh: 并且把它讲给别人听，看看它是否能产生共鸣。

speaker: Unknown
en: I remember the number of times I told the Nest story before Nest came out because I was like, "This is crazy, this thermostat thing."
zh: 我记得在 Nest 出来之前我讲了多少次 Nest 的故事，因为我想，“这太疯狂了，这个恒温器的东西。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Okay, let me set up the virus of doubt, which was, do you know how much you spend on your energy bill every year for your heating and cooling?
zh: 我想，“好吧，让我先制造怀疑的种子，就是，你知道你每年在供暖和制冷的能源账单上花多少钱吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't you hate your thing?"
zh: 你难道讨厌你的那个东西吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, there's another way to look at the storytelling and this is, and I put this in the book.
zh: 现在，看故事讲述还有另一种方式，这，我把这写在了书里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, no, I didn't put this in the book.
zh: 哦，不，我没把这个放在书里。

speaker: Unknown
en: We all have seen infomercials, right?
zh: 我们都看过信息型广告，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Those ones that drag on for a half an hour, an hour at late night TV on some random, on every channel now, right, and they just sit there and they tell you the story from all the different directions.
zh: 那些在深夜电视上拖沓半小时、一小时，现在每个频道都有，随机播放的那些，对吧，他们就坐在那里，从各个不同的角度给你讲故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: They give you the virus of doubt.
zh: 他们给你植入怀疑的种子。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're like, "Oh, here's this cheese grater.
zh: 他们就像，“哦，这里有个这个擦菜板。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you know when you have this bad cheese grater here and your knuckles bleed," and whatever and it's hard to clean.
zh: 你知道当你有个这个糟糕的擦菜板，你的指关节出血，”诸如此类，而且很难清洗。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they over exaggerate everything.
zh: 然后他们夸大其词。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they show you all the pain points of it and then they show how wonderful, easy it is to use in this whatever they're selling and then it's easy to buy, and then it's easy to return, and it's overly dramatic.
zh: 他们向你展示所有的痛点，然后展示这东西是多么美妙、易于使用，不管他们在卖什么，然后很容易购买，然后很容易退货，而且非常戏剧化。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we all know it's like wrestling, it's all an act and whatever.
zh: 我们都知道这就像摔跤，全是在演戏，诸如此类。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's probably no realness there, but it is storytelling in a way.
zh: 那里可能没有什么真实性，但这也是一种讲故事的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: And not that you should do that because they over-hype the expectations, but look at the techniques that are used, the psychological techniques, the emotional techniques to get there and then dial it back and go, "Okay, I'm going to do that but with truth."
zh: 并不是说你应该那样做，因为他们过度夸大了预期，但看看所使用的技巧、心理技巧、情感技巧，然后把它回调，说，“好吧，我要那样做，但带着真相。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's when things start to go pop, pop, pop, pop, or at least that's what I've seen.
zh: 那就是事情开始开始爆发的时候，或者至少我是这么看到的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I always use that as another metric.
zh: 所以我总是把这作为另一个指标。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's not just telling the story over and over, it's what story you are telling.
zh: 这不仅仅是一次又一次地讲故事，而是你在讲什么故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I try to think of that as well, but not obviously clowny and cheesy and everything else, but it is a caricature of doing good marketing.
zh: 所以我也尽量考虑到这一点，但显然不要滑稽和俗气以及其他所有东西，但这确实是做好营销的一种漫画表现。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like this is a really interesting lens into the press release first or working backwards idea, is create the infomercial first and just go extreme.
zh: 我觉得这是审视“新闻稿优先”或“逆向工作”理念的一个非常有趣的视角，就是先制作一个信息型广告，然后做到极致。

speaker: Tony Fadell
en: Sure, you could.
zh: 当然，你可以。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh my God.
zh: 天哪。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just kept up with a whole new-
zh: 我只是刚刚想到了一个全新的——

speaker: Unknown
en: Know what you don't want to do for sure.
zh: 你肯定知道你不想做什么。

speaker: Lenny
en: But it's like a cool lens to go really far and then kind of pull back the best parts and make it honest. I think we just came up with a really good idea here. I want to come back to the iPhone. So I think it-
zh: 但这就像一个很酷的视角，可以走得非常远，然后稍微找回最好的部分并让它诚实。我觉得我们在这里刚刚得出了一个非常好的主意。我想回到 iPhone。所以我认为它——

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Lenny
en: Came out 20 years ago almost at this point, right? It launched 27, 2007. Something like that.
zh: 差不多在这一点上发布于 20 年前，对吧？它在 2007 年 6 月 27 日发布。差不多是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, 2007.

speaker: Unknown
en: So what's crazy is people are still trying to build the next iPhone, especially with AI.
zh: 所以疯狂的是人们仍然在试图建立下一个 iPhone，尤其是随着 AI 的出现。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone's just like, "We've had this thing forever.
zh: 每个人都像，“我们这个东西已经有一辈子了。

speaker: Unknown
en: What the hell?
zh: 到底怎么回事？

speaker: Unknown
en: Why has nothing changed?"
zh: 为什么没有什么改变？”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm curious, I imagine people ask you this question.
zh: 我很好奇，我想人们会问你这个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm curious just what you think the next iPhone may look like with AI.
zh: 我只是好奇你认为有了 AI 的下一个 iPhone 会是什么样子。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the device that you think emerges over time?
zh: 你认为随着时间推移会出现什么设备？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you have vision?
zh: 你有愿景吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's the what it could look like long-term and there's what it could look like soon.
zh: 这有它长期可能的样子，也有它近期可能的样子。

en: And so a lot of people are like, okay, there's the long-term thing and when we can trust the models, and when they have memory, and when they do this stuff, then we're still going to need a display because, sorry people, unless we're plugging it into our brain like a BCI brain computer or there's some laser thing going into our retina, we're going to need a display.
zh: 所以很多人会说，好的，有那个长期的东西，当我们能信任这些模型，当它们有记忆，当它们做这些事情时，那时我们仍然需要一个显示屏，因为抱歉人们，除非我们要把它像 BCI 脑机接口那样插进我们的大脑，或者有什么激光玩意儿射进我们的视网膜，我们需要一个显示屏。

speaker: Unknown
en: So barring those kinds of technologies, we're going to need a display.
zh: 所以除非那些技术，否则我们需要一个显示屏。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the best display that we have is a smartphone-like thing.
zh: 所以我们要拥有的最好的显示屏是类似智能手机的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Not going to be this tiny thing.
zh: 不会是这种小东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: We saw what happened with Humane, all that other stuff.
zh: 我们看到了 Humane 身上发生了什么，以及所有其他那些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you're going to have some kind of, because the best way to visualize visual information is with a display, right?
zh: 所以你会有某种，因为可视化视觉信息的最好方式是用显示屏，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: So we're going to have some kind of small slab, maybe it's foldable like we see today, whatever, so that you can access it.
zh: 所以我们要有某种小平板，也许像我们今天看到的可折叠的，随便吧，这样你可以访问它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe many things you don't need a display for, but a lot of things you still do, right?
zh: 也许很多事情你不需要显示屏，但很多事情你仍然需要，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because you might not be tapping, and swiping, and all that stuff.
zh: 因为你可能不再点击和滑动以及所有那些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I am of the opinion that long term, if you look at how a device is layered today, and this is many, many devices, and iPhone specifically started this, which was tapping and swiping, right?
zh: 所以我认为长期来看，如果你看看今天设备是如何分层的，这是很多很多设备，特别是 iPhone 开启了这个，那是点击和滑动，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: That was the first thing, is use your finger.
zh: 那是第一件事，就是用你的手指。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then after that was keyboard and then after that, the tertiary thing was a voice input.
zh: 在那之后是键盘，然后再之后，第三位的是语音输入。

speaker: Unknown
en: We need to flip it.
zh: 我们需要把它翻转过来。

speaker: Unknown
en: We need to absolutely flip it and we have to say, and this is what I always wanted to do with Nest, which is I want to remove displays and we need to have voice as the number one primary feature and you build around voice.
zh: 我们需要彻底把它翻转过来，我们必须说，这也是我一直想用 Nest 做的事情，也就是我想移除显示屏，我们需要把语音作为第一主要特性，然后围绕语音来构建。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then we have keyboard if necessary and then we have tapping and swiping.
zh: 然后我们有键盘，如果必要的话，然后我们有点击和滑动。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It should go exactly the opposite.
zh: 它应该正好相反。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the problem why most of us don't go around talking to our phones and everything, and we saw this also happen with cars.
zh: 而为什么我们大多数人不会四处对着我们的手机说话做所有事情的问题，我们在汽车上也看到这种情况发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: There was tactile buttons, and then there was the touchscreens, and then they also added the voice in, but nobody really uses voice in the car unless it's some accessibility thing, right, because voice was always added at the end because it was always like, well, it sort of works and it's a gizmo, but it's like Alexa was for version 1.0 or Siri was [inaudible 01:08:55].
zh: 曾经有实体按键，然后有触摸屏，然后他们也加进了语音，但没人在车里真正使用语音，除非是某种无障碍功能，对吧，因为语音总是最后加进去的，因为它总是像，好吧，它勉强能用，它是个小玩意，但它就像 Alexa 是 1.0 版本或者 Siri 是 [听不清 01:08:55]。

speaker: Unknown
en: But when we actually have really good voice input with not just understand it's like whisper flow or something because that's great.
zh: 但当我们真正拥有非常好的语音输入，不仅仅是理解它，就像 Whisper flow 之类的东西，因为那很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what I'm saying is the intelligence behind it with memory and everything else.
zh: 而我要说的是，它背后的智能，带有记忆和所有其他东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So then we can start to say and we can start to use that much more specifically and then deprecate those other things, but we've always have to have them there as a crutch for voice because voice has never been able to deliver.
zh: 所以那时我们可以开始说，我们可以开始更具体地使用它，然后弃用那些其他东西，但我们总是不得不把它们保留在那里作为语音的拐杖，因为语音从来没有能够兑现。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's the long-term thing.
zh: 所以那是长期的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it'll have some kind of display, but it's going to be much more voice primary and then you're going to have the other things as secondary and tertiary.
zh: 所以它会有某种显示屏，但它将是更加以语音为主，然后你要把其他东西作为第二和第三。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the middle now or sometimes soon, it's going to look much, much like the smartphone is because we're not going to get away from the all app interface anytime soon.
zh: 在中期现在或者有时很快，它看起来会非常像智能手机，因为我们不会很快摆脱全应用界面。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to remove some things, but we don't trust it yet.
zh: 我们会移除一些东西，但我们还不信任它。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's going to take a while till we trust it.
zh: 我们要花一段时间才能信任它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we are literally turning over a lot to this trusted thing, right?
zh: 而我们真的正在把很多东西交给这个受信任的东西，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because tapping and swiping, we know what that is, for the most part.
zh: 因为点击和滑动，我们大部分知道那是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: We know that we can trust ourselves when we're tapping and keyboarding or whatever.
zh: 我们知道当我们点击和敲键盘或其他什么时，我们可以信任我们自己。

speaker: Unknown
en: This other thing, we don't know.
zh: 这个其他东西，我们不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so it's going to take a lot of time, reminds me of kind of general magic in a way, for us to be able to get en masse to trust it.
zh: 所以这需要很多时间，这让我以某种方式想起 General Magic，为了让我们能够大规模信任它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we're already seeing this with coding agents and everything else, "Oh, they deleted my source code."
zh: 而我们已经看到了与编码智能体和所有其他东西相关的情况，“噢，他们删除了我的源代码。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, I'm not trying to be an old guy who's saying it's not going to work.
zh: 而且，我不是要做一个说这不会奏效的老顽固。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sure, it's going to work in certain things.
zh: 当然，它在某些事情上会奏效。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm saying for consumers every day and the things they want, and it's cheap enough so it doesn't cost them a lot, right?
zh: 我是说对于每天消费者和他们想要的东西，而且它足够便宜所以不会花费他们很多，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Today people are like, "Yeah, I'm going to try ChatGPT or whatever.
zh: 现在人们会想，“是的，我要试试 ChatGPT 或随便什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's $20 a month or $200 a month."
zh: 它是每月 20 美元或每月 200 美元。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That is unsustainable if you think consumers are going to pay that.
zh: 如果你认为消费者要付那个钱，那是不可持续的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's just no way unless it's incredible, but they're not going to... You know what I mean?
zh: 根本不可能，除非它 incredible，但他们不会…… 你懂我的意思吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: They tried it and now they're getting kind of their Siri, a lot of them are getting their Siri 1.0 with this.
zh: 他们试过了，现在他们正有点得到他们的 Siri，他们很多人正随这个得到他们的 Siri 1.0。

speaker: Unknown
en: "I paid for it, but it's really not all that yet."
zh: “我为此付了钱，但它真的还没那么厉害。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's like full self-driving.
zh: 这就像 full self-driving。

speaker: Unknown
en: I paid for full self-driving, it's 15 years later, I'm still waiting for full self-driving.
zh: 我付了 full self-driving 的钱，15 年后，我还在等待 full self-driving。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think we, again, understand what we think we want, but where the technology is and the social adoption and the social trust that needs to be created around that is going to take a lot of time and it's going to take a lot of iterations to get there, and it's especially if you're going to have to pay for it.
zh: 所以我认为，再次，我们理解我们认为我们想要什么，但技术所在的位置，以及社会采用，以及需要围绕此建立的社会信任，将需要很多时间，并且需要很多次迭代才能达到那里，特别是如果你必须为此付费。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's really interesting to hear you say that long-term even we're going to need a screen because I think a lot of people are trying to go to just like an AirPod or some kind of magical AI.
zh: 听你说长期甚至我们需要一个屏幕真的很有趣，因为我认为很多人试图只走向一个 AirPod 或某种神奇的 AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sure.
zh: 当然。

speaker: Unknown
en: How are you going to look at a map if you want to look at a map?
zh: 如果你想看地图，你要怎么看地图？

speaker: Unknown
en: You're going to listen, like put on the voice stuff in your car and never look at the map on your car and say, "Oh, turn left in 200 feet.
zh: 你会去听，比如在车里打开语音提示，从来不用看车里的地图，而是说，“噢，200 英尺后左转。

speaker: Unknown
en: Turn left in 100 feet."
zh: 100 英尺后左转。”

speaker: Unknown
en: You're like, "Shut up.
zh: 你就会说，“闭嘴。

speaker: Unknown
en: I can't hear you anymore.
zh: 我再也听不见你说话了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just want to glance over."
zh: 我只想瞄一眼。”

en: So I don't buy it, like unless you have it jacked in your brain or into your eye or some other way, it's getting into your cortex.

en: For people that don't know what you do these days, you talked about all these companies you work with, help people understand what it is you spend your time on, in case they may want to try to work with you on this stuff.
zh: 对于那些不知道你这些天在做什么的人，你谈到了你合作的所有这些公司，帮助人们了解你把时间花在什么地方，以防他们可能想尝试在这些事情上与你合作。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the first thing to know is that we invest in deep technology.
zh: 我认为首先要知道的是我们投资于深层技术。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that could be hardware, it could be software, software plus hardware, it could be chemical, it could be all kinds of different stuff.
zh: 所以这可能是硬件，可能是软件，软件加硬件，可能是化学，可能是各种不同的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: But remember, we talked earlier about what's the pain and is there new technology that comes out to solve that pain in a new way?
zh: 但请记住，我们之前谈到了痛点是什么，是否有新技术出来以新的方式解决那个痛点？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so what we do is I've learned that I invest in the deep technologies that are going to unseat the incumbents because it's going to change the market or the product in such a dramatic way that customers will choose this.
zh: 所以我们所做的是，我了解到我投资于那些将取代现有巨头的深层技术，因为它将以如此戏剧性的方式改变市场或产品，以至于客户会选择这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we're not just competing on features or better marketing or whatever.
zh: 所以我们不仅仅是在功能或更好的营销或诸如此类的事情上竞争。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're fundamentally a different product.
zh: 我们是一个根本上的不同产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now it might take longer to get the market to shift to it, but it's fundamentally different, just like I brought up Grok and Cerebras and these kinds of things.
zh: 现在可能需要更长的时间才能让市场转向它，但它是根本上的不同，就像我提到的 Grok 和 Cerebras 以及这类事情一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's fundamentally different.
zh: 它是根本上的不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so at Build, what we do is we invest in those technology companies that can be that seed that can unseat things, and maybe they go the distance, or maybe they are the key enabling tech that allows another startup to go to the distance, that kind of stuff.
zh: 所以在 Build，我们所做的是投资于那些可以成为取代事物的种子的技术公司，也许它们能走完全程，或者也许它们是关键使能技术，允许另一家初创公司走完全程，这类事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's what we invest in, and we do it in the environment, in societal benefits, and health benefits.
zh: 所以这就是我们投资的，我们在环境、社会效益和健康效益方面做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's where we focus, and at times we've had a portfolio of over 200 companies, and we do that, but we don't just invest.
zh: 这就是我们关注的重点，有时我们的投资组合有超过 200 家公司，我们这样做，但我们不仅仅是投资。

speaker: Unknown
en: When we get involved, many of the companies we advise and that we come in help them with product management, operations, we help them with financing, we help them with org development, just all kinds of different ways that... A lot of times in marketing and communications, right?
zh: 当我们参与时，我们要建议的许多公司，我们进入帮助它们进行产品管理、运营，我们帮助它们进行融资，我们帮助它们进行组织发展，只是各种不同的方式……很多时候在营销和传播方面，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because we talked about storytelling, storytelling is really important, and a lot of deep technology people, they're incredible engineers, scientists, researchers, what have you.
zh: 因为我们谈到了讲故事，讲故事真的很重要，许多深层技术的人，他们是令人难以置信的工程师、科学家、研究员，等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: They have a really good idea, but they don't necessarily know how to form the product around it, or form the marketing around the product they're building, or help the marketing to inform the product and vice versa.
zh: 他们有一个非常好的想法，但他们不一定知道如何围绕它形成产品，或围绕他们正在构建的产品形成营销，或帮助营销来告知产品，反之亦然。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we try to encapsulate that and really bring that to bear so they don't hit it on the fourth version.
zh: 所以我们试图封装这一点并真正利用它，这样他们就不会在第四个版本上才碰壁。

speaker: Unknown
en: They try to get very close to the first or second version so they can get on that three version cycle to get to a great company, and so that's what we do.
zh: 他们试图非常接近第一个或第二个版本，这样他们就能进入那个三个版本的循环以成为一家伟大的公司，这就是我们所做的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we get to have a lot of fun.
zh: 所以我们有很多乐趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: We get to word play in all these different spaces: health, medicine, drugs, robotics, chips, these kinds of things.
zh: 我们可以在所有这些不同的领域进行文字游戏：健康、医学、药物、机器人技术、芯片，这类事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's just what's wonderful to us, and we get to just be kids in the candy store and work with all these incredible, smart entrepreneurs and everything.
zh: 所以这就是对我们来说如此美妙的事情，我们就像糖果店里的孩子一样，与所有这些令人难以置信的、聪明的企业家一起工作，等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's one thing I do.
zh: 所以这是我做的一件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: The other thing I do is I'm a designer at residence at MIT.
zh: 我做的另一件事是我是麻省理工学院的常驻设计师。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I just finished up my first year there with the MIT Media Lab in architecture and design, and help to give lectures and work with students, amazing, amazing, smart students with the great technology, but help them with the customer journey.
zh: 所以我刚刚在那里完成了我在麻省理工学院媒体实验室建筑与设计专业的第一年，帮忙举办讲座并与学生合作，那些非常、非常聪明的学生，他们掌握着伟大的技术，但我帮助他们理解客户旅程。

speaker: Unknown
en: Try to make sure that they see this stuff early on in their career, not after 10 years, and then they finally understand, oh, wait a second, what am I really building?
zh: 试着确保他们在职业生涯早期就能看到这些东西，而不是在 10 年之后，他们才终于明白，哦，等一下，我到底在构建什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Who is it for?
zh: 它是给谁的？

speaker: Unknown
en: Not just what is it am I building, but why am I building it, and for who?
zh: 不仅仅是我正在构建什么，还有我为什么要构建它，以及为谁构建？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, trying to get that into some of the later undergraduate and graduate students so we can watch them change the world more quickly.
zh: 所以，试图将这一点灌输给一些高年级本科生和研究生，这样我们可以看着他们更快地改变世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: Incredible.
zh: 难以置信。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love just all the levels of ways you are helping people.
zh: 我喜欢你帮助人们的所有层面和方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's the investing, there's this book, there's MIT, there's these conversations.
zh: 这包括投资、这本书、麻省理工学院，以及这些对话。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's fun.
zh: 这很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just so much fun.
zh: 这真的太有趣了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love how much fun you're having.
zh: 我很欣赏你玩得这么开心。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there anything else you wanted to share that we didn't cover?
zh: 还有什么其他你想分享但我们要没覆盖到的内容吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: We should really just talk about ethics and morals and your point of view on that.
zh: 我们真的应该谈谈伦理和道德，以及你对此的观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And as a product manager, as a product designer, you need to really consider these things.
zh: 作为产品经理，作为产品设计师，你需要真正考虑这些事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I understand we have lots of big questions about AI and is it going to be a disaster for the next generation and for societies and all the other stuff?
zh: 我明白我们有很多关于 AI 的大问题，它会不会成为下一代和社会的灾难，以及其他所有的东西？

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think that you really need to be well grounded and have real principles when you're designing something, and don't let those things go astray, just like you wouldn't go astray with a bad user interface or something like that.
zh: 但我认为你在设计某样东西时，真的需要脚踏实地并有真正的原则，不要让那些事情偏离正轨，就像你不会因为糟糕的用户界面或其他类似的东西而偏离正轨一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Make sure you're not trying to addict your users.
zh: 确保你不是试图让你的用户上瘾。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if anybody is, there's always other jobs, and there's always other better companies, and don't chase the money for tearing apart what it is the fabric of this society that we built.
zh: 如果有人在这样做，总是有其他工作，总是有其他更好的公司，不要为了赚钱而去撕裂我们所建立的社会结构。

speaker: Unknown
en: Obviously, there's going to be innovation, and there's going to be change, but when you're really doing it, and you can see that you're doing it, and you're trying to get people hooked, or you're like, "Oh, I give more dopamine or what have you."
zh: 显然，会有创新，会有变化，但当你真的在做这件事，而且你能看到你在做这件事，并且你试图让人们上钩，或者你就像这样，“哦，我要提供更多的多巴胺或者其他什么。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That's when you have to start really looking at things.
zh: 那就是你必须开始真正审视事情的时候了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And a lot of times, people are young, and they think, "Oh, that's great.
zh: 很多时候，人们都很年轻，他们想，“哦，那太好了。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's what I want."
zh: 那就是我想要的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But when you start to have kids, and you start to have families, and you start to see the... And you're not just about you; you're not trying to just get out of your family when you were growing up.
zh: 但是当你开始有了孩子，开始有了家庭，开始看到……而且你不再只是关于你自己；你不再像在你成长过程中那样试图摆脱你的家庭。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm an individual, and I want what I want.
zh: 我是一个个体，我想要我想要的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: You really need to start thinking systemically about the benefit that you're bringing to this society as a whole, not just to the business that you're in and trying to bring in whatever revenue you're trying to do because at the end of the day, users will feel that and then I think and will reward that as we see with Apple with privacy and people say, "Oh, they're behind because they're so private and there's this."
zh: 你真的需要开始系统地思考你给整个社会带来的利益，而不仅仅是给你所在的企业，并试图带来 whatever 你试图做的收入，因为在一天结束时，用户会感觉到这一点，然后我认为并会奖励这一点，正如我们在 Apple 和隐私方面看到的那样，人们说，“哦，他们落后了，因为他们太注重隐私，还有这那的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's a double-edged sword.
zh: 但这是一把双刃剑。

speaker: Unknown
en: I remember when we had a very pointed discussion when the iTunes music store and when it was turning into video was being specified and what we were thinking about it, and everyone's like, "Oh, this is going to be great.
zh: 我记得当我们进行非常尖锐的讨论时，当时 iTunes 音乐商店和当它转变为视频时正在被制定规格，以及我们在考虑它，每个人都说，“哦，这会很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to do movies.
zh: 我们要做电影。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to do TV shows.
zh: 我们要做电视剧。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's going to all be great.
zh: 那一切都会很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to make a lot of money with this in the studios.
zh: 我们要在工作室里靠这个赚很多钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everybody's behind it."
zh: 每个人都支持它。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then somebody goes, "Yeah, and we should have porn, of course."
zh: 然后有人说，“是的，当然我们应该有色情内容。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Steve was like, "What?
zh: Steve 就说，“什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that the kind of world you want your kids to grow up in?
zh: 那是你希望你的孩子长大的那种世界吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And Apple is related with that and Apple's about... Is that what we want to do?"
zh: Apple 与之相关联，而且 Apple 是关于……那是我们想要做的吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was very clear, it was shut down, and we need leaders like that.
zh: 很显然，这被否决了，我们需要那样的领导者。

speaker: Unknown
en: We need leaders who are very clear as opposed to "I'm going to make a huge service for everyone."
zh: 我们需要非常明确的领导者，而不是那种“我要为每个人做一个巨大的服务”。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they're all sex chatbots for everyone.
zh: 而且它们都是给每个人的性爱聊天机器人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just think when you start normalizing those things, and you start telling other generations that's a value and you start... And I understand that everyone has their own what's right and what's wrong.
zh: 我只是认为当你开始将那些事情常态化，你开始告诉其他几代人那是一种价值，你开始……我理解每个人都有自己的是非观。

speaker: Unknown
en: And here I am, the old guy, again, I think we can go a little too far.
zh: 现在我又成了那个老家伙了，我认为我们可能有点走得太远了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm for individual rights and for everyone to be a thing, but when major companies are doing this in the guise of certain kinds of behaviors they're trying to achieve, when we're selling... We're turning personal connection into a product with AI chatbots and making them really devolve social interaction to the point where I'm going to have a perfect interaction with this thing because the world is so messy, it's like we're losing humanity with that, and we're just for gain.
zh: 我支持个人权利，支持每个人成为某种样子，但是当大公司以试图实现某种特定行为为幌子来做这件事时，当我们在销售……我们正在用 AI 聊天机器人将个人联系变成产品，并使社会互动真正退化到我将与这个东西进行完美的互动的地步，因为世界是如此混乱，这就像我们正在失去人性，而且我们只是为了利益。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I wish that the other product designers out there really take it to heart.
zh: 所以我希望那里的其他产品设计师真的把这一点记在心里。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not saying you got to be Lily White and being in a church every weekend and Bible banging.
zh: 我并不是说你必须纯洁无瑕，并且每个周末都去教堂和敲打圣经。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not saying that; I'm not MAGA.
zh: 我不是那个意思；我不是 MAGA。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not any of that stuff, just saying, hey, think about it.
zh: 我不是任何那种东西，只是说，嘿，想一想。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of people look at the iPhone as this thing now that everyone's hooked on.
zh: 很多人把 iPhone 看作是现在每个人都沉迷的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so good.
zh: 太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're just like, it's too good.
zh: 我们只想说，这太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's all this data showing all the impact it has on people and things like that.
zh: 有很多数据显示了它对人们的所有影响以及诸如此类的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just how do you think about that and the impact the iPhone has had on people?
zh: 你是怎么看那个的，以及 iPhone 对人们产生的影响？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, the first thing is the iPhone wasn't set out to be that.
zh: 嗯，首先，iPhone 并不是为了那样而设立的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's unintended consequences, and the unintended consequence was social media happened, and social media, Apple's not a social media company, but it does distribute the apps or make available those apps.
zh: 有意外的后果，而这个意外的后果是社交媒体发生了，而且社交媒体，苹果不是一家社交媒体公司，但它确实分发那些应用程序或使那些应用程序可用。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the way I think about it is we have lots of junk food, and we have an obese nation or obese world because of all the junk food, and it takes us to regulate our consumption to do that and get healthy tools.
zh: 所以我对它的看法是，我们有很多垃圾食品，我们有一个肥胖的国家或肥胖的世界，因为所有的垃圾食品，这需要我们要调节我们的消费来做到这一点并获得健康的工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's nutritional elements on the back of things.
zh: 而且在物品的背面有营养成分元素。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you want to make a better decision, you can, and I hope AI and these assistants will help people be able to get better control of those kinds of things where our lizard brain is being stimulated in a way that consume more, consume more of physical food.
zh: 如果你想做出更好的决定，你可以，我希望 AI 和这些助手能够帮助人们能够更好地控制这类事情，因为我们的蜥蜴脑正在受到刺激，以一种消耗更多、消耗更多实物的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: And now the same thing's happening in digital food, but we have digital food that doesn't have the nutrition labels, doesn't have the warnings, doesn't have the regulation that it needs to have, just like we do with our physical food because, oh, you can't let innovation, whatever, be slowed down.
zh: 现在同样的事情正在数字食品上发生，但我们的数字食品没有营养标签，没有警告，没有它需要的监管，就像我们对待我们的实物食品一样，因为，哦，你不能让创新，不管什么，被减速。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not saying again, being a nanny and all the other stuff, but we got to have some balance, and it's just swung too far.
zh: 我并不是再说做一个保姆以及所有其他的事情，但我们必须有一些平衡，而现在只是走得太远了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I still think that the platform companies like Google and Apple could be doing a lot more around digital consumption tools and information to help people make better decisions for themselves, for their families, and what have you, because you could go to the... iPhone's just a refrigerator, and you can put in junk food or you can put in good food.
zh: 我仍然认为像谷歌和苹果这样的平台公司可以在数字消费工具和信息方面做得更多，以帮助人们为他们自己、为他们的家庭以及其他类似情况做出更好的决定，因为你可以去…… iPhone 只是一个冰箱，你可以放垃圾食品，也可以放好的食物。

speaker: Unknown
en: And even if you put in good food, you could go to the good food every five seconds.
zh: 即使你放了好的食物，你也可以每五秒钟就去拿好的食物。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have a refrigerator in your kitchen.
zh: 你厨房里有一个冰箱。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can put good food or bad food.
zh: 你可以放好的食物或坏的食物。

speaker: Unknown
en: You could open it all the time and keep consuming.
zh: 你可以一直打开它并继续消费。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we need to learn habits.
zh: 所以我们需要学习习惯。

speaker: Unknown
en: We need to teach habits.
zh: 我们需要传授习惯。

speaker: Unknown
en: We need to put in regulations.
zh: 我们需要制定法规。

speaker: Unknown
en: We need information.
zh: 我们需要信息。

speaker: Unknown
en: We need tools to help us monitor and manage that stuff, and they need to be supplied.
zh: 我们需要工具来帮助我们监控和管理那些东西，它们需要被提供。

speaker: Unknown
en: And just like we have rules around who can buy something when they're 21 or under 21.
zh: 就像我们有关于谁可以买东西的规则，当他们 21 岁或 21 岁以下时。

speaker: Unknown
en: We need all those kinds of things, and it needs to happen because why?
zh: 我们需要所有这些类型的事情，它需要发生，因为为什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Sure, you could have a short-term gaining of customers, but if you make your customers unhealthy, you're not going to have customers.
zh: 当然，你可以获得客户的短期增长，但如果你让你的客户变得不健康，你就不会有客户了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let me just say for folks that haven't read your book, I just want to communicate how great it is.
zh: 我只是想对那些没读过你书的人说，我想传达一下它有多棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's very few books that are both tactically useful that give you, like, here's how to do a thing and also just inspire you to build and make great things.
zh: 很少有书既在战术上有用，给你像这就是如何做一件事的方法，同时又激励你去建造和创造伟大的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Build does that in such a great way.
zh: Build 就是这样以一种很棒的方式做到的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think everyone knows about it.
zh: 我想大家都知道它。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if everyone's read it.
zh: 我不知道是否每个人都读过它。

speaker: Unknown
en: So highly encourage you to read it.
zh: 所以强烈鼓励你去读它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Two final questions.
zh: 最后两个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Where can folks find you online if they want to maybe learn about the stuff you're up to now, and how can listeners be useful to you?
zh: 如果人们想了解你现在的动向，他们可以在哪里在线找到你，以及听众怎么能对你有用？

speaker: Unknown
en: Great.
zh: 很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, let's see.
zh: 嗯，让我们看看。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, thanks again.
zh: 嗯，再次感谢。

speaker: Unknown
en: This was a fun interview.
zh: 这是一个有趣的采访。

speaker: Unknown
en: I really enjoyed it.
zh: 我真的很享受。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think if you want to interact with us, we're at buildc.com.
zh: 我想如果你想与我们互动，我们在 buildc.com。

speaker: Unknown
en: So Build Collective, but we're BuildC, B-U-I-L-D-C.com.
zh: Build Collective，但我们是 BuildC，B-U-I-L-D-C.com。
